REPLY BRIEF IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO STRIKE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES (TRANSACTION ID # 61389541) FILED BY DEFENDANT BUILD GROUP, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BUILD GROUP, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS' PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AGAINST BUILD GROUP. DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE CAMPOS' ALLEGATIONS THAT BUILD GROUP REPEATED THE MISREPRESENTATION ABOUT COMPLIANCE WITH THE Q235 STANDARD THAT WAS TOLD TO IT AND WILLFULLY AVOIDED VERIFYING THE ACCURACY OF THE REPRESENTATION ARE INSUFFICIENT TO SHOW THAT BUILD GROUP ACTED WITH MALICE, FRAUD OR OPPRESSION TO SUPPORT IMPOSITION OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES AGAINST BUILD GROUP. IF THE CAMPOS ARE ABLE IN GOOD FAITH TO ALLEGE ADDITIONAL FACTS SHOWING THAT BUILD GROUP ACTED WITH MALICE, FRAUD OR OPPRESSION, THEY ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO DO SO. MR. RAMOS INFORMS THE COURT THAT PLAINTIFF DECLINES LEAVE TO AMEND AT THIS TIME. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: VICKI GROSS, CSR 5525 (EMAIL: VGROSS@SFTC.ORG)
MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-01-2017 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

Defendant Build Group Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs' Prayer For Punitive Damages Against Build Group
Off Calendar Dec-01-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BUILD GROUP, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS' PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AGAINST BUILD GROUP

Appearances: Julio J. Ramos (415.948.3015) for plaintiff and responding party; Jonathan C. Hsieh of Christensen Ehret (916.503.4094) for defendant and moving party.

Discussed and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED with twenty days leave to amend. The Campos' allegations that Build Group repeated the misrepresentation about compliance with the Q235 standard that was told to it and willfully avoided verifying the accuracy of the representation are insufficient to show that Build Group acted with malice, fraud or oppression to support imposition of punitive damages against Build Group. If the Campos are able in good faith to allege additional facts showing that Build Group acted with malice, fraud or oppression, they are given leave to do so. Mr. Ramos informs the Court that Plaintiff declines leave to amend at this time. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Vicki Gross, CSR 5525 (email: vgross@sftc.org) (302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-05-2017 Continued To Dec-20-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
